scope: Full project architecture summary (compute + storage + scripts)

architecture_overview.goal: Near-Data Processing for BPE tokenization in a disaggregated storage environment using NVMe passthrough (opcode 0xD4).
architecture_overview.environment: Host issues custom NVMe commands; data is processed on storage server/runtime; data does not round-trip to host.
architecture_overview.core_idea: Host computes file LBA extents and sends NVMe passthrough; storage/runtime consumes LBA ranges and performs tokenization.

major_components.name: compute (host-side)
responsibilities: Resolve file extents via FIEMAP and convert to NVMe LBA segments.
Submit NVMe passthrough commands via io_uring (opcode 0xD4).
Provide Python bindings (pybind11) for host control scripts.
Optional host-side Arrow metadata processing for extent-index generation.

key_modules.notes: Converts filesystem extents to {slba, nblocks} segments.
FIEMAP -> io_uring pipeline; outputs to .bin file.
Exports tokenize_to_nvme() and arrow_text_dump(); .arrow input triggers extent-index path internally.
Host-side Arrow IPC -> text conversion (CLI + pybind).
Maps Arrow text column byte-ranges to LBA extents without exporting to Python.

major_components.name: storage/runtime (storage-side)
responsibilities: Consume shared memory buffers and message queue requests.
Run BPE tokenization on storage server side.
Maintain runtime stats in shared memory.

key_modules.notes: SysV shm + msg queue; slot-based request handling.

major_components.name: storage/spdk
responsibilities: SPDK NVMe-oF target tooling and scripts (external subtree).

major_components.name: scripts (experiments/host workflows)
responsibilities: Download dataset (OpenWebText) and save in Arrow format.
Run sharded tokenization experiments and metrics.

key_scripts.purpose: Download OpenWebText and save to Arrow shards.
Shard-level tokenization experiments with timing metrics.

txt_path: Host gets FIEMAP extents for TXT file.
Host sends NVMe passthrough (opcode 0xD4) for LBA ranges.
Storage/runtime reads raw bytes and tokenizes in-place.
arrow_path_recommended: Host parses Arrow metadata to locate text column byte-ranges.
Host sends LBA extents via NVMe passthrough.
Storage/runtime processes LBA ranges without Arrow parser.

current_implementation_stage.python_api: tokenize_to_nvme now accepts .arrow and internally uses extent-index path; extent-index is not exposed to Python.
current_implementation_stage.runtime_arrow_parser: Not implemented (by design; storage/runtime stays lightweight).
current_implementation_stage.padding_handling: Assumed handled in SPDK/runtime; extent-index includes block_offset + byte_length for precise trimming.

known_gaps_and_risks: Extent-index currently relies on Arrow buffers being mmapped zero-copy to compute file offsets.
Row-level reconstruction requires offsets buffer handling if strict row boundaries are needed.
Sparse files or holes can cause FIEMAP coverage gaps; current extent-index treats uncovered ranges as errors.
recommended_next_steps: Define a stable on-disk extent-index schema (JSON or binary).
Add runtime consumption of extent-index (LBA + byte trimming).
Add tests comparing TXT pipeline vs Arrow+extent-index pipeline.
